anchors: preserve the same defining silhouette, proportions, and identifying traits across all shots
keep the lead subject emotionally legible from shot to shot
maintain wardrobe, fur, markings, or props unless the story explicitly changes them
avoid sudden species, costume, or age drift
preserve the same companion or counterpart identity across all shots
keep relative scale and relationship cues stable
maintain distinctive visual traits, props, or markings
avoid random role or appearance changes

worldAnchors: Temperate rainforest, moss-covered stone, storm light, cedar roots
lens language alternates between intimate detail and wider spatial orientation
repeat environment motifs and color continuity so shots feel part of one world

promptStructure.systemPrompt:
You are a short-drama workflow planner for text, image, and video generation.
Produce outputs that are cinematic, emotionally coherent, and practical for downstream media models.
All scene assets must preserve character identity, wardrobe, props, and environment continuity.
Final target: runtime = 180 seconds, resolution 1280x720, episodic short-drama pacing.
promptStructure.storyPrompt:
BRIEF: A bear and a dog become unlikely friends after surviving a storm together.
TITLE: Shelter
LOGLINE: A lost bear cub and a stray dog forge an unlikely bond while surviving a violent storm in an ancient forest.
GENRE: Naturalist Drama
TONE: Tender, survivalist, contemplative
TARGET_RUNTIME_SECONDS: 180
PRIMARY_CHARACTER: Young brown bear cub
SECONDARY_CHARACTER: Stray border collie mix
VISUAL_WORLD: Temperate rainforest, moss-covered stone, storm light, cedar roots
TASK: Write a shot-by-shot short-drama outline with escalating stakes, emotional reversals, and a final visual resolution.
OUTPUT FORMAT: JSON with story package plus scenes, each scene including prompt and audio only.
promptStructure.imagePromptTemplate:
[BRIEF]
[STORY_ANCHORS_AND_WORLD_STYLE]
[SHARED_SCENE_PROMPT]
[LIGHTING_AND_COLOR]
[CONSISTENCY_RULES]
[NEGATIVE_CONSTRAINTS: no contradictory details, no random replacement, no text overlays]
promptStructure.videoPromptTemplate:
[APPROVED_KEYFRAME_REFERENCE]
[SHARED_SCENE_PROMPT]
[NATIVE_AUDIO_DIRECTION: diegetic ambience and effects only]
[ENVIRONMENT_CONTINUITY]
[DURATION_AND_RESOLUTION_TARGET]
[FAILURE_AVOIDANCE: preserve key visual forms and recurring elements, avoid sudden morphs or unrelated insertions]
promptStructure.continuity:
Primary anchor summary: preserve the same defining silhouette, proportions, and identifying traits across all shots; keep the lead subject emotionally legible from shot to shot; maintain wardrobe, fur, markings, or props unless the story explicitly changes them; avoid sudden species, costume, or age drift.
Secondary anchor summary: preserve the same companion or counterpart identity across all shots; keep relative scale and relationship cues stable; maintain distinctive visual traits, props, or markings; avoid random role or appearance changes.
World anchors: Temperate rainforest, moss-covered stone, storm light, cedar roots; lens language alternates between intimate detail and wider spatial orientation; repeat environment motifs and color continuity so shots feel part of one world.
Operational rule: each shot gets one hero keyframe, and its video job launches once that shot is ready for the queue.
If a key subject, object, or recurring visual form changes unexpectedly across neighboring shots, issue an image-edit correction pass before launching the corresponding video clip.
promptStructure.sampleImagePrompt: Scene 1 keyframe for "Shelter". Brief: A bear and a dog become unlikely friends after surviving a storm together. World style: Temperate rainforest, moss-covered stone, storm light, cedar roots Character anchor: Young brown bear cub Secondary anchor: Stray border collie mix Shared scene prompt: Extreme close-up of rain-soaked border collie fur trembling, dog backed into hollow cedar log interior, Pacific Northwest forest, pre-dawn grey light filtering through entrance, shallow depth of field on water droplets Lighting and palette: cinematic realism with coherent contrast, location-appropriate color, and continuity across the full short. Consistency rules: preserve the core visual identity, scale logic, defining forms, and recurring elements across shots; avoid random replacement, contradictory details, or continuity drift.
promptStructure.sampleVideoPrompt: Generate a 720p cinematic video clip for scene 1 of "Shelter". Start from the approved keyframe image and preserve its core visual elements and continuity. Shared scene prompt: Extreme close-up of rain-soaked border collie fur trembling, dog backed into hollow cedar log interior, Pacific Northwest forest, pre-dawn grey light filtering through entrance, shallow depth of field on water droplets Motion direction: preserve the action, framing, and continuity implied by the shared scene prompt with natural motion blur and coherent movement. Pacing direction: movement and camera timing should support a 15-second clip with clean entry and exit frames for concat. Native audio direction for shot 1: Heavy rain, wind through trees, distant thunder Keep sound realistic and synchronized to motion. Avoid voice-over, exact scripted dialogue requirements, dialogue subtitles, or unrelated effects. Safety constraints: stable forms, stable recurring elements, no sudden background swaps, no subtitle burn-in, and no unrelated intrusions.

endpointPlan: Step 1: user enters one brief; app calls Kimi 2.5 to derive a shot list sized to the target runtime.
Step 2: app submits shot keyframes first, with conservative retry and backoff.
Step 3: after keyframes are ready, shot video jobs are queued in small batches against the official DashScope video endpoint.
Step 4: each video prompt includes diegetic audio direction and optional short human vocal presence so Wan 2.5/2.6 can return clips with sound already embedded.
Step 5: server-side FFmpeg normalizes each clip to 1280x720 and concatenates the final MP4.
assemblyChecklist: Shot video fallback order: wan2.6-i2v -> wan2.6-i2v-flash.
Target clip duration: 15s on the Wan 2.6 path.
Clip audio comes directly from the video model; there is no separate narration track in the production path.
Every finished clip is normalized to 1280x720 before final concat.
Final deliverable: 1280x720 H.264 MP4 with audio.
concreteExecution: Execution pattern: keyframes run first; video starts after keyframes succeed and flows through a bounded queue.
Result URLs consumed in-chain: keyframe URL -> native-audio video clip -> final assembly
